body_txt: A trope concerning any setting where a faction is waging a war they have no hope of winning. The faction is up against impossible odds and any victory they manage to achieve is just postponing their inevitable defeat . The losing side may believe Losing Is Worse Than Death and continue fighting out of a sense of honor or patriotism. They may keep the war going because they've given too much to just lose now. A more heroic version involves dragging out their defeat for the greater good, sacrificing its soldiers to buy time for its civilian population to evacuate or to prepare for a Heroic Second Wind . More often than not, the war might have been intended as a minor engagement before things went FUBAR , got complicated, and then went completely to hell, and now everybody is SOL. It's basically the war equivalent of Kick the Dog , Shoot the Dog and Shoot the Shaggy Dog ... and of course War Is Hell . In this bleak situation there's bound to be Despair Event Horizon , I Did What I Had to Do , Black-and-Gray Morality , or Grey-and-Gray Morality . All for the sake of victory, but Was It Really Worth It? Beware bad writing, for it can turn this into prime fuel for Too Bleak, Stopped Caring . A Forever War may eventually grind itself down to one of these. Lowered Recruiting Standards may go along with this trope when things get truly desperate. On a smaller scale, Last Stand . See also Bad Future , and Shocking Defeat Legacy .
